title: UK personal-loan application with ID verification, document upload, and interruption recovery
productContext: Fictional UK consumer credit web app (product name 'Lendflow') taking a borrower from an indicative quote through a full unsecured personal-loan application: identity verification, affordability assessment, and document upload. The application is multi-step and frequently interrupted: applicants close the tab, lose connectivity, or let the session idle past its timeout. The flow MUST recover without data loss: partially-entered application data must survive a session timeout and be re-presentable on return, never silently discarded. The full application triggers a HARD credit search and this must be disclosed before the customer consents. The product is fictional and is not FCA-authorised; the brief deliberately does not supply a guaranteed decision turnaround, a document list, or an affordability threshold. Every URL in this brief is synthetic and uses example.com.
users: applicant working through the full loan application for the first time
returning applicant resuming an in-progress application after an interruption
applicant on a metered or unstable mobile connection uploading identity and affordability documents
jobs: complete identity verification and document upload without losing progress if I am interrupted
understand that proceeding past the consent step will trigger a hard credit search that affects my credit file
resume my application exactly where I left off after a session timeout, browser close, or connectivity loss
see which required documents are still outstanding without re-uploading anything I already provided
requiredJourneys: applicant consents to a hard credit search with clear disclosure before the hard search is triggered
returning applicant resumes an interrupted application and finds every previously entered field and uploaded document intact
applicant experiences a session timeout and is routed to a recovery flow that preserves entered data rather than wiping the form
constraints: Proceeding past the consent step triggers a HARD credit search that will appear on the customer's credit file; the hard-search disclosure MUST precede consent and must not be bundled into generic terms-and-conditions prose.
Partially-entered application data MUST survive a session timeout and be re-presentable on return; silently discarding entered data on timeout is forbidden.

The flow MUST support interruption recovery: an applicant who closes the tab, loses connectivity, or idles past the timeout must be able to resume exactly where they left off, including uploaded documents.

No guaranteed decision turnaround, document list, or affordability threshold is supplied in this brief; these must surface as clearly-templated placeholders or dynamic pulls, not as fabricated figures.
The hard-search disclosure must not be darkened: pre-ticked consent, consent bundled with marketing preferences, or consent as a prerequisite for seeing the application summary are all forbidden.
The product name Lendflow is fictional; do not imply FCA authorisation, regulation, or affiliation with any real lender, credit reference agency, or identity-verification vendor.

mobileRules: the draft-autosaved indicator stays visible long enough to be read on a narrow viewport and is not a transient toast that vanishes
primary continue action sits in the bottom thumb zone and remains operable in a single-handed portrait grip
the hard-search disclosure text remains readable at the smallest breakpoint without horizontal scrolling
the consent control must not be pre-ticked and must sit separate from any marketing-preference control
an interrupted upload MUST be resumable from where it stopped, not restarted from zero, on a metered mobile connection
the list of outstanding documents stays visible alongside uploaded documents so the applicant can see what remains
